text:
“Urban and suburban service” means a service performed in urban or suburban areas, or between municipalities in close proximity, except that:

(a) The one-way route mileage of the service shall not be more than 50 miles.

(b) Designated motor coach routes over state highways outside limits of incorporated cities where the one-way route mileage is over 25 miles, but does not exceed 50 miles, shall be approved by the Department of Transportation.
markdown:
# Vehicle Code
## Division 15. SIZE, WEIGHT, AND LOAD [35000 - 35796]
### Chapter 2. Width [35100 - 35111]
##### § 35107

“Urban and suburban service” means a service performed in urban or suburban areas, or between municipalities in close proximity, except that:

(a) The one-way route mileage of the service shall not be more than 50 miles.

(b) Designated motor coach routes over state highways outside limits of incorporated cities where the one-way route mileage is over 25 miles, but does not exceed 50 miles, shall be approved by the Department of Transportation.

*(Amended by Stats. 1974, Ch. 545.)*

subsections.text: “Urban and suburban service” means a service performed in urban or suburban areas, or between municipalities in close proximity, except that:

subsections.label: a
subsections.text: The one-way route mileage of the service shall not be more than 50 miles.

subsections.label: b
subsections.text: Designated motor coach routes over state highways outside limits of incorporated cities where the one-way route mileage is over 25 miles, but does not exceed 50 miles, shall be approved by the Department of Transportation.
